---
title: "Safeguadring confidential data"
---

The problem with secure and confidential data is that if leaked to AI models,
it can lead to data breaches and privacy violations bearing substantial legal
and reputational risks. We must remember that it is **our responsibility to**:

1. control of what data is shared with the AI.
2. review and curate the context AI uses.
3. properly anonymize, aggregate, or simulate data before sharing with AI.
4. follow organizational policies and legal requirements for responsible
   AI use [ai/](https://ai.worldbank.org/).

::: callout-important
Please familiarize yourself with the [World Bank's AI Use Policy](https://ai.worldbank.org/policy) and [Responsible AI Guidelines](https://ai.worldbank.org/guidelines) to ensure compliance and responsible use of AI in your work.
:::

## Key data exposure channels

In general, **AI does not load raw data into context as is**. However,
as it develops code to understand the data, it can potentially
propose doing things that lead to data exposure. Based on literature [^1]
most common channels of data exposure are:

[^1]: Yan, B., Li, K., Xu, M., Dong, Y., Zhang, Y., Ren, Z., & Cheng, X. (2024). *On Protecting the Data Privacy of Large Language Models (LLMs): A Survey*. arXiv:2403.05156. <https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.05156>

1. **user-written prompts that include sensitive information**,
2. **AI suggests code that prints raw data as Stata output in the console**,
3. **AI instructs IDE to read text files or load them into context**
4. **AI proposes command line code that searches specific text in the data files (e.g. `.csv` files) or load them into context**.

In any of these cases, sensitive data becomes part of the context that AI uses
to generate code and outputs, and thus **always there is a risk of data leakage**.

::: callout-note

In principle and depending on the AI provider, each chat session with AI is an
isolated instance of a foundational model. Data shared with it my not be used
for training, but it may be retained for a certain period of time. However,
**every movel provider has a different**
**policies on data usage, retention, and training**. If you need please
familiarize yourself with:

1. [Positron Assistant Provider Privacy Information](https://positron.posit.co/assistant-provider-info.html) and
2. [Responsible use of GitHub Copilot inline suggestions](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/responsible-use/copilot-code-completion), and
3. [Responsible use of GitHub Copilot Chat in your IDE](https://docs.github.com/en/copilot/responsible-use/chat-in-your-ide)

:::

The problem with secure and confidential data is that if leaked to AI models,
it can lead to data breaches and privacy violations bearing substantial legal
and reputational risks. We must remember that it is **our responsibility to**:

1. control of what data is shared with the AI.
2. review and curate the context AI uses.
3. properly anonymize, aggregate, or simulate data before sharing with AI.
4. follow organizational policies and legal requirements for responsible
   AI use [ai/](https://ai.worldbank.org/).

::: callout-important
Please familiarize yourself with the [World Bank's AI Use Policy](https://ai.worldbank.org/policy) and [Responsible AI Guidelines](https://ai.worldbank.org/guidelines) to ensure compliance and responsible use of AI in your work.
:::


## Remedies

### Think what you write in the prompts

Be careful with what you write in the prompts, and avoid sharing any sensitive
information in them. For example, instead of sharing raw data values, share
variable names, types, summary statistics, and data dictionaries to provide
AI with necessary context without sharing raw data values.


::: callout-warning
Never share with AI any passwords, personally identifiable information, or
any other sensitive data in the prompts.
:::

### Instruct IDE to exclude certain files from context

1. Instructing IDE to exclude certain files from context.


### Instruct AI explicitly to not read specific files
2. Instruct AI explicitly to not read specific files (also using a command line tools).
3. Using Metadata‑based AI Assistance.
   - Move sensitive data outside of the working folder.
   - Locate in the working folder only metadata files that contain variable
     names, types, labels, summary statistics, missing observations, and data dictionaries.

4. Anonymizate / pseudoaggregate data.
5. Develop code that never prints raw data in the console.

6. Metadata‑Driven AI Assistance.

   - Use AI to generate code that produces **textual metadata** and summary
     of your data set that contains: Variables names, types, labels, summary
     statistics, missing observations, and dictionaries. This allows you to
     leverage AI's capabilities for data understanding and code generation
     without sharing examples of the raw data.
     AI will use this metadata to generate code for data cleaning, validation,
     and harmonization, while the actual data remains secure on your local
     machine and you decide whether or not to execute with AI in Positron or
     in Stata without Positron's context aggregation.

7. Anonymization and Aggregation.

      for  to provide AI with necessary context without sharing raw data. For example, share variable names, types, summary statistics, and data dictionaries instead of actual data values.
